_comment_env: Runtime env vars (PAPER_DOWNLOAD_DIR, UNPAYWALL_EMAIL, ARXIV_STORAGE_PATH, OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH, OBSIDIAN_API_KEY) are inherited from the parent process -- set them with `setx FOO BAR` and re-open your shell BEFORE launching Claude Code. We don't put them in `env: {}` here because Claude does not expand %FOO% placeholders when it spawns MCP servers; static values would freeze the config. The %USERPROFILE% paths above ARE expanded at install time by install-mcp-servers.ps1.
_comment_obsidian: The obsidian server reads OBSIDIAN_API_KEY and OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH from the inherited environment via mcp-obsidian. Make sure those are set with setx before first launch.
_comment_chrome: chrome-devtools-mcp is the paywall-bypass tool. It launches a real Chrome with a persistent profile so the user signs into their library proxy / SSO once and the cookies persist across sessions. The --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled flag defeats Cloudflare Turnstile's basic automation detection. Replaces the legacy university-paper-access (IP-only fetch, silently saved paywall HTML on failure) and scihub (Windows charmap encoding bug, legally grey) MCPs from earlier pack versions. See skills/deep-research/references/paywall_workflow.md for the per-paper recipe.
_comment_chrome_prereq: Requires Chrome stable installed and the user to log into their institutional library proxy ONCE in the chrome-devtools-mcp profile (the first time the MCP is invoked, Chrome opens visible; user navigates to their EZproxy login URL). After login, downloads are automated.
